Title: It's back to work for Dr M [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Prime Minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad has returned to his office at Yayasan Kepimpinan Perdana in Putrajaya, nearly three months after sustaining a hip fracture in a fall at his home.
A video posted on Mahathir's official Facebook page today shows him arriving at the foundation's premises with a police escort, before being seen in his office reading a newspaper, sitting in a meeting and signing documents.
The caption reads, "Back to the office, March 25, 2026."
The text accompanying the video carries the phrase, "As long as you can..."
The post drew an outpouring of well wishes, with many Malaysians expressing admiration for Mahathir's resilience regardless of their political views.
View this post on Instagram
A post shared by Dr Mahathir Mohamad (@chedetofficial)
"I may not be agreeing all the time with what he says and does. But I can't deny his perseverance and resilience in doing what he does. Salute," wrote user Allison Por Li Fang.
"Nothing will stop Tun from working," wrote Lisa Sani, while Aman Virk offered, "He is living because he is working. Keep working."
User Ahmad Zulqurnain Ghazalli said, "'As long as you can' is a phrase of reflection for the next generation."
On Jan 10, it was reported that Dr Mahathir is expected to undergo a lengthy recovery process following a
hip fracture
sustained in a fall at his home around 7.30am on Jan 6.
Since then, he has made several public appearances, including
a brief visit to a cafe
on Jan 27 and a tour of the newly refurbished Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad with his wife on the eve of Hari Raya on March 21.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ramanan denies he is 'Mr R', challenges Victor Chin to name him
Article:
SHAH ALAM: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has denied allegations linking him to controversial businessman Victor Chin and the alleged "corporate mafia".
The Sungai Buloh member of parliament challenged Chin to substantiate his accusations and not keep the public guessing over the identity of "Mr R", describing the claims as baseless and part of a broader attempt to discredit the government.
"Just name the person. Stop playing victim and using dirty narratives to tarnish PKR and the government, if you have the evidence, show!," he said during a press conference at his office here today.
Ramanan, who is the Human Resources Minister, questioned whether Chin's intentions in making the allegations were to bring down the government, adding that he does not know Chin, and has never met or spoken to him.
"Later today, my division chiefs will be lodging police reports against Chin," he said, adding that it is most apparent Chin is blaming enforcement agencies because he wants asylum in the United Kingdom.
Ramanan dismissed claims linking him to an alleged RM9.5 million "corporate mafia" deal, saying it was not related to him, although he too was curious about the identity of the individual involved.
He reiterated he does not hold any public shares to date, noting such transactions by elected representatives would fall under scrutiny by regulators such as the Securities Commission Malaysia.
He also questioned Chin's credibility, pointing to what he described as shifting allegations involving multiple enforcement agencies, including Bank Negara Malaysia, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission and the police.
"Every day is a different story. Why are we giving credibility to such an individual?" he said.
Ramanan maintained he would cooperate fully with authorities and urged Chin to return to Malaysia to face investigations.
"This is a country governed by law. If you are innocent, come back and defend yourself. Don't sit overseas and make wild accusations," he said, branding Chin a "Corporate Devil".
Ramanan said he called for the press conference to clear PKR's name, amid what he described as damaging narratives surrounding the "Mr R" allegations by Chin.
He also said remarks by the party's former deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli on the issue — including through podcasts and social media — had contributed to confusion and doubt, making it necessary for party leaders to respond.
"When the party is attacked in this manner and such implications are made, I have a duty as vice-president of the party to make sure this is clear. If we remain silent, people will begin to believe it is the truth," he said.
Ramanan said Rafizi's statements could be misconstrued as factual if left unchallenged.
"When he speaks and we do not respond, it creates the impression that what he says is true. So, somebody needs to speak up," he said.
He also challenged those making allegations — including Rafizi — to name the individuals.
Chin has emerged at the centre of a growing controversy involving allegations of a so-called "corporate mafia" and an ongoing police investigation.
Chin, who is linked to business interests associated with NexG Bhd — the sole supplier of Malaysia's passport booklets and identity cards — is being investigated under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (Amla) over alleged links to the group. He has denied any involvement.
Chin claimed he and his associates were instead subjected to sustained pressure by the group, alleging it attempted to gain control of NexG through intimidation, including threats and repeated demands for the "delivery of shares".
He alleged instructions related to these demands were conveyed through an intermediary, identified as "Mr R", whom he claimed is an MP linked to PKR.
Chin also alleged "Mr R" had significant influence, including the ability to intervene in enforcement matters.
According to Chin, he sought assistance from "Mr R" to address both the investigation and his issues with the alleged group.
He claimed "Mr R" requested RM10 million in October last year in exchange for resolving the matter.
Chin said he paid RM9.5 million but that his issues remained unresolved. He has since demanded a refund and warned he would publicly expose the individual if the matter is not settled.
Authorities are continuing their investigation, while Chin — who is believed to be abroad — maintains that he has done no wrong.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CAP: Make plastic-free water bottles compulsory for students amid heatwave
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Consumers' Association of Penang (CAP) has urged the Education Ministry to make it compulsory for schoolchildren to bring plastic-free water bottles to school to cope with the current hot weather.
Its education officer, N.V. Subbarow, said this measure was necessary to ensure students remain hydrated and protected.
"Only a small number of students bring water bottles to schools, while many others do not, often because they do not have one."
Malaysia is experiencing a
heatwave, which is expected to persist until June
. Temperatures are predicted to peak between mid-March and mid-April.
Subbarow said students should be encouraged to drink more water during the heat spell, describing plain water as the best remedy against heat-related risks.
He urged teachers to play a role by ensuring students brought water to class and finished their supply before leaving.
"The ministry must take preventive measures to safeguard schoolchildren. They should not simply be left to sit under fans, they should be taught how to cope with oppressive heat."
He said some students spent money on sweet carbonated drinks instead of drinking water, underscoring the need for better awareness.
He said assistance should be extended to students from low-income families.
"If students or their parents are unable to afford plastic-free bottles, the ministry should provide them, possibly under the Rahmah initiative."
He stressed that urgent action was needed to protect children from exposure to dangerously high temperatures.
"We must act now to safeguard schoolchildren during this period of excessive heat."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-Sabah minister Peter Anthony released on parole after forgery conviction
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Sabah infrastructure minister Datuk Peter Anthony has been released on parole after serving part of a three-year jail sentence for a forgery conviction.
The Prisons Department confirmed the matter to the New Straits Times today that he was granted release under the Licensed Release of Prisoners Programme and is now in Sabah.
He is understood to have been released two weeks ago.
Peter was sentenced on May 26, 2022,
to three years' imprisonment and a RM50,000 fine after being found guilty of using forged documents to secure a system maintenance contract.
At the time, he was managing director of Asli Jati Sdn Bhd. He was convicted of falsifying a letter from the Universiti Malaysia Sabah deputy vice-chancellor's office dated June 9, 2014, by inserting a false statement with intent to deceive.
The offence was committed at the Office of the Chief Private Secretary to the Prime Minister at Perdana Putra in Putrajaya between June 13 and Aug 21, 2014.
On Aug 20, 2025,
the Court of Appeal upheld his conviction and sentence,
dismissing his final bid to overturn the ruling.
Peter later filed a revision application challenging the appellate court's decision, which had affirmed the sentence imposed by the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court.
He won the Melalap state seat in 2018 and 2020 under Parti Warisan, before founding Parti Kesejahteraan Demokratik Masyarakat in 2022.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police call for tougher laws, upgrades after attacks on stations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have called for stronger laws and improved infrastructure at critical facilities following a series of attacks on police stations in recent years.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said incidents such as the fatal shooting of an officer in Pinggiran, Subang in 2017 and the 2024 Ulu Tiram attack, which left two policemen dead, underscored the growing boldness of those challenging law enforcement.
"These incidents clearly show that there are parties who dare to challenge the law and authority in this country. It is also an insult to enforcement institutions," he said in his Police Day address.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tengku Zafrul: Asean chip race intensifies, Malaysia firmly in the mix
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Southeast Asia's semiconductor race is intensifying, with Malaysia firmly in the mix, said Malaysian Investment Development Authority chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz.
He said Malaysia remains among Asean's key semiconductor players, backed by its long-standing strength in assembly, test and packaging, which accounts for about seven per cent of global capacity.
"What matters now, however, is the shift underway. We are seeing increasing movement into higher-value segments," Tengku Zafrul said in a LinkedIn post.
He said this is supported by expansion from global players such as Infineon and Intel, alongside clear policy direction through the National Semiconductor Strategy, as well as strong demand from artificial intelligence, data centres and electrification.
The shift comes as Southeast Asia intensifies efforts to carve out a larger role in the global semiconductor supply chain amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and supply chain diversification.
Tengku Zafrul, who is also senior political advisor to the Prime Minister, said the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone presents a structural opportunity to strengthen Malaysia's position by combining complementary advantages with Singapore.
Malaysia offers manufacturing scale, a growing talent pipeline and cost competitiveness, while Singapore brings strengths in research and development, chip design, capital and global connectivity.
"If executed well, this is not a zero-sum dynamic. It is an opportunity to expand the region's semiconductor ecosystem and Malaysia's role within it," he said.
Beyond industry positioning, he said the broader goal is to create high-value jobs, enable local firms to move up the value chain and build capabilities that will underpin long-term economic resilience.
"The question is no longer whether Malaysia participates in the semiconductor race. It is how far up the value chain we can move, and how quickly," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR will not shield any of its members - Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PKR will not shield any of its members, including its members of Parliament, who are found to be involved in misconduct such as corruption.
Its information chief, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, said this aligns with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's message that no group will be shielded from accountability for misconduct.
Fahmi said PKR is ready to cooperate with the authorities following allegations involving a party member in the misappropriation of funds worth RM10 million.
"The PKR leadership has so far been informed about news reports containing statements and allegations from the individual concerned.
"Therefore, we fully leave it to the authorities to carry out further investigations if any wrongdoing is found.
"We have nothing to hide and PKR will cooperate with the enforcement authorities," he told reporters after the 219th Police Day celebration today.
Yesterday, businessman Victor Chin reportedly claimed that he handed over RM9.5 million to a member of Parliament from PKR to help him resolve issues surrounding a "corporate mafia" scandal that has been troubling him.
Chin, in a 40-page document detailing the chronology of the events, said he only provided RM9.5 million.
However, he said that the remaining RM500,000 was never handed over to the MP because the matter was not resolved.
Fahmi, who is the communications minister, added he had no information regarding the identity of the PKR MP linked to the allegations.
"There is no need for PKR to lodge a police report regarding these allegations. It is up to the complainant to make a report, whether to the police or any enforcement agency.
"Any investigation process will begin with an official report from the complainant, and this is a procedure that must be followed before further action is taken," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to chart best- to worst-case risks in six-month outlook
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Cabinet has directed the Home Ministry and police to prepare a comprehensive analysis of geopolitical, economic, and security risks.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the study would include scenario planning ranging from best- to worst-case outcomes.
"It will cover geopolitical, economic, and security scenarios, including what may happen if current global developments continue over the next six months," he told reporters after the national Police Day celebrations today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi ordered to file defence in RM10mil defamation suit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli has been ordered by the High Court to file his defence in a RM10 million defamation suit filed against him by social media influencer Ariz Ramli, better known as Caprice.
Ariz's lawyer, Mohd Rezan Ezra, said the Pandan member of parliament and former economy minister was instructed to do so by senior assistant registrar Nur Shasha Hidayah Nor Azahar during case management conducted via e-review today.
Also in the proceedings was Rafizi's lawyer, Edmund Choi.
Rezan said Rafizi has also confirmed receipt of the summons through him and has filed a memorandum of attendance.
"The court has ordered Rafizi to file his defence on or before April 9.
"Meanwhile, Ariz will file his reply to the defence on or before April 30, " said Rezan.
He added that the next case management has been fixed for May 13.
Ariz, 40, as plaintiff, filed the suit on Feb 26, claiming that Rafizi's statements in four videos linking him to an alleged RM2.5 billion corruption to the National Energy Transition Roadmap project were defamatory.
He said the statements damaged his reputation and he has been exposed to hatred, contempt and ridicule.
He said he has suffered financial losses in the estimated sum of RM10 million as loss of current and future earnings, including business opportunities.
Ariz is seeking RM10 million in special damages from Rafizi, 49, along with general, exemplary, punitive and aggravated damages deemed appropriate by the court.
Additionally, he is seeking an injunction to restrain Rafizi from posting or circulating the videos as well as an unconditional apology.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Enforcement must be paired with engagement on 3R issues
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Firm enforcement must be complemented by engagement to manage sensitive issues involving race, religion, and royalty, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said.
He said recent incidents — including religious disputes and statements that offended certain communities — highlighted the risks such issues pose.
"Once such issues are triggered, they are difficult to control and can have harmful effects on society," he told reporters after the national Police Day celebrations today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Cops must stay 'steps ahead' as crime grows more complex
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police must remain "two to three steps ahead" of evolving crime and security threats, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said.
He warned that the security landscape is becoming increasingly complex, citing cases where police officers themselves have been targeted.
Investigations have uncovered structured criminal networks with multi-layered hierarchies, involving youths as young as 17 or 18 recruited and directed by older operatives, some based overseas.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Authorities know Victor Chin's whereabouts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Authorities know the whereabouts of businessman Victor Chin, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said.
Declining to elaborate, he told reporters: "We know where he is."
Saifuddin said police would first determine the direction of the investigation, identify the modus operandi and then call in those involved.
He added that authorities would proceed methodically and would not rush into measures such as travel restrictions or passport seizures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia targets top 10 in Global Peace Index within five years
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is aiming to break into the top 10 of the Global Peace Index (GPI) within five years, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said.
He said Malaysia is currently ranked 13th out of 165 countries, placing it among the top three most peaceful nations in the Asia-Pacific, behind Singapore and New Zealand, and second in Asean.
"This achievement did not happen by default, but by design, through a clear national security policy and the role of the police as the country's primary law enforcement agency," he said after the 219th Police Day celebration today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Differing analyst views shape Sunway's IJM Corp takeover offer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Research firms are largely supportive of Sunway Bhd's takeover offer for IJM Corp Bhd, but significant differences in valuation remain among independent analysts.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd, which maintains an "Accept" recommendation, said the offer provides an opportunity to exit at a premium in a challenging market environment.
MBSB Research and TA Securities also view the offer as fair, while RHB Research maintains a "Buy" call and CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd places a higher valuation of RM3.50 per share.
The differences reflect varying valuation assumptions and views on the appropriate timing for investors.
The offer price of RM3.15 per share represents a premium to the prevailing market price, giving investors an immediate opportunity to lock in gains.
However, independent valuations suggest IJM's intrinsic value may be higher, supported by its concession assets, landbank and construction order book.
This presents a familiar dilemma for investors: whether to accept immediate gains or hold for longer-term upside.
Some market observers point to a potential "best of both worlds" approach, allowing investors to balance short-term returns with future value.
Sunway's entry as a significant shareholder is also seen as a potential catalyst, which could accelerate project execution and value creation.
Institutional positioning remains a key factor, with major shareholders such as Permodalan Nasional Bhd and the Employees Provident Fund reportedly not supportive of the offer.
External factors, including developments related to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission, have also weighed on sentiment.
Ultimately, the decision hinges on investors' assessment of current value versus future potential.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MIC stays with Barisan Nasional - Saravanan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MIC will remain in Barisan Nasional, deputy president Datuk Seri M. Saravanan said at a press conference at the party's headquarters today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar tasks IGP, Home Minister with new security strategies amid global uncertainty
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for new strategies to safeguard national security, warning that complacency could leave the country vulnerable amid growing global uncertainty.
Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebrations today, Anwar urged Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail to formulate fresh approaches to ensure security remains under control.
He cautioned that shifting geopolitical tensions were creating "signs of desperation at all levels", underscoring the need for constant vigilance.
"We must remain vigilant and prepared. In any theory, whether by Sun Tzu or Clausewitz, all without exception, remind us of the need for heightened alertness when external conditions are uncertain," he said.
Anwar warned that the greatest mistake nations make is becoming too comfortable and assuming peace is guaranteed.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia steps up energy security efforts amid Strait of Hormuz risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim chaired the third 2026 National Economic Action Council meeting to review geopolitical developments, global economic pressures and security implications following the Middle East conflict.
In a Facebook post today, he said that although the country is still in the Hari Raya Aidilfitri festive period, the people's well-being, especially the cost of living, remains the Madani government's priority.
He said the government will implement early, proactive and firm measures immediately to ensure economic resilience.
Anwar said the government is stepping up efforts to secure energy supply, including diversifying sources and exploring alternative routes to reduce reliance on high-risk routes such as the Strait of Hormuz.
He added that authorities will continue to monitor the situation closely and share information transparently with the public.
"In facing these challenges, calmness, discipline and unity are important to ensure the country remains stable and resilient," he said.
Anwar has previously urged consumers to spend prudently and exercise financial discipline.
The conflict in the Middle East has triggered a severe disruption to the global energy system, cutting supplies and forcing consumers worldwide to face higher prices and reduced consumption.
The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow channel off Iran's coast, has halted the passage of about 20 per cent of global oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) since US and Israeli air strikes on Iran began on Feb 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OE EduGroup chairman sets MBR entrepreneurship participation record
Article:
PETALING JAYA:
OE EduGroup chairman Datuk Wira Dr Calvin Khiu has been recognised by the Malaysia Book of Records (MBR) for attracting the highest number of participants in an entrepreneurship programme.
The recognition was presented during MBR's 30th anniversary celebration at One World Hotel on Friday, attended by more than 800 guests, including government officials and corporate leaders.
Khiu earned the title "Most Attendees in an Entrepreneurship Programme" after his programme recorded over one million participants across online and physical platforms.
MBR said the achievement highlights the programme's large-scale impact in advancing practical entrepreneurship learning nationwide.
Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said such records serve as a source of inspiration.
"Each record represents the spirit of pushing boundaries and striving for excellence," she said.
MBR chairman Datuk Michael Tio said the organisation has recognised more than 8,800 national records and published 17 editions of its record books since its establishment in 1995.
Speaking to the media, Khiu said his interest in entrepreneurship education was shaped by observing that many people hold back due to perceived limitations in background or resources.
"In reality, the difference lies in mindset and the willingness to find a way forward," he said, adding that clarity of purpose plays a crucial role in sustaining one's journey.
Reflecting on his early experience in the curtain business, he stressed the importance of focus and commitment, even when presented with opportunities in other sectors.
As his business stabilised, he turned to education, seeking to fill a gap in structured learning for aspiring entrepreneurs, particularly in business models, organisational culture and presentation skills.
On facing challenges, he advised staying composed and solution-oriented.
"Challenges are inevitable, but beyond every difficulty lies an opportunity. As long as we continue moving forward, solutions will emerge," he said.
Looking ahead, Khiu outlined plans to build a comprehensive entrepreneurship ecosystem in Malaysia and across Asean over the next decade.
He explained that the ecosystem would encompass multiple stages of growth. It would begin with education to nurture talent, followed by industry integration to create value, incubation to scale businesses, media to build influence, and ESG integration to ensure long-term sustainability.
Through this integrated approach, he hopes to empower more individuals, particularly young entrepreneurs, to recognise opportunities and confidently chart their own path to success.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'It wasn't me': Rafizi denies RM9.5m claim
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations that he received RM9.5 million from businessman Victor Chin, amid mounting speculation online.
In a social media post, Rafizi, who is also Pandan member of parliament, dismissed the claim in a light-hearted tone.
"It wasn't me," he wrote, joking that while he may have "gained some weight over Hari Raya", he did not receive RM9.5 million.
The denial follows claims and threats by Chin to reveal the identity of the alleged recipient unless the money is returned.
In a separate post, Rafizi said the issue raised more serious concerns, particularly over how authorities respond to such allegations.
He warned it would be dangerous if enforcement agencies were aware of alleged wrongdoing but took actions that could inadvertently facilitate criminal activity.
"At that stage, it is no longer about turning a blind eye. It has entered the realm of collusion," he said.
Rafizi was responding to Chin's assertion that investigations should not focus solely on him, but also involve other parties, including those allegedly using the Anti-Money Laundering Act (Amla) to exert pressure.
He said such claims were serious, as they suggested that elements within institutions tasked with combating crime could instead be part of a broader illicit ecosystem.
"When illegal activities reach tens of billions, small-scale protection is no longer enough. They require influence, position, and individuals who can open doors. That is where politics becomes involved," he said.
Rafizi added that the issue went beyond any single individual, pointing to a wider intersection between illicit funds, enforcement, and politics.
"When that happens, the country becomes highly vulnerable to compromise," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia resilient as fertiliser crunch hits Southeast Asia crops
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The risk of reduced application rates for 2026/2027 grain crops across Southeast Asia is increasing, particularly as key application windows approach, posing downside risks to production, according to BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions.
In a statement today, it said Malaysia is among the most insulated from nitrogenous fertiliser supply constraints, given its strong domestic production capacity, which should help limit downside risks to application rates, along with Indonesia and Vietnam.
"The effectiveness of this insulation will depend on policy efforts to prioritise domestic supply amid attractive export opportunities.
"We believe the region is more uniformly exposed to constraints in phosphatic fertiliser supplies due to its reliance on imported inputs," said BMI.
It added that since the escalation of the conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran on Feb 28, 2026, global urea prices have risen sharply.
The US Gulf New Orleans granular urea spot index increased by 40.4 per cent from Feb 27, 2026, to close at US$66 per tonne on March 20, 2026, reflecting expectations of significantly tighter global fertiliser supply amid constrained exports from the Gulf Cooperation Council region, it said.
The region accounted for around 20 per cent of global nitrogenous fertiliser exports by value in 2024 and supplies a significant share of the natural gas used in fertiliser production.
"With grain planting seasons approaching across South East Asia, sustained high prices for nitrogen-based fertilisers, including urea, on which grain production is highly reliant, could prompt under application, raising downside risks to 2026/2027 crop yields," it said.
BMI expects impacts to be uneven across markets, reflecting differing production structures, policy responses and exposure to imports.
Beyond nitrogenous fertilisers, it expects South East Asia to face heightened risks from the phosphatic fertiliser supply chain.
Thailand and Malaysia rely more heavily on alternative suppliers, including Egypt, which accounts for approximately 90 per cent and 50 per cent of their phosphatic fertiliser imports, respectively.
"Though this reduces exposure to China‑specific supply risks, both markets remain vulnerable to higher prices stemming from tightening global supply conditions and elevated logistical and transport costs," said BMI.
Beyond fertiliser‑related challenges, it highlights increasing downside risks to grain output across South East Asia stemming from expected El Nino conditions.
"As of March 2026, the Climate Prediction Centre assigns a greater than 60 per cent probability of El Nino conditions emerging from June, with 48 per cent chance of at least moderate intensity by August, signalling increasing weather risks amid the crop development period broadly across the region," said BMI.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia ranked 13th safest globally, second in Asean - IGP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has climbed to 13th place in the Global Peace Index 2025, reflecting improved security in the country, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today.
He said the achievement places Malaysia as the second safest country in Asean, driven by a 6.4 per cent reduction in index crimes and the implementation of more than 9,000 security operations nationwide.
Despite several isolated incidents during the review period, Khalid said the overall security situation in the country remained good and under control.
Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebration at the Police Training Centre here today, he said the police would continue strengthening their capabilities to meet growing public expectations and address increasingly complex security challenges.
"The police are now operating in a highly challenging environment. We must undergo paradigm shifts to remain relevant and meet the expectations of the government and society," he said.
Also present was Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Deputy Inspector General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay.
He stressed that criticism and public perception would not deter the force from upholding justice and enforcing the law firmly.
Khalid said the police would continue to anchor their operations on national security priorities, including safeguarding sovereignty, maintaining socio-political stability, strengthening governance, ensuring public safety and enhancing international recognition.
He also highlighted the need for stronger legal frameworks and infrastructure upgrades at critical police facilities following past attacks on police stations, including the 2024 Ulu Tiram incident which left two officers dead.
"These incidents show there are parties bold enough to challenge the law and authority. This requires a more comprehensive approach, including improvements to existing laws to keep pace with current threats," he said.
Khalid said the police would intensify efforts to strengthen professionalism and accountability through better training, monitoring, enforcement and the adoption of artificial intelligence and advanced technologies.
He also expressed appreciation for continued government support, noting that RM12.063 billion had been allocated to the police this year, alongside an additional RM200 million to enhance IT infrastructure at police stations and quarters nationwide.
Khalid said the theme "Police and community are inseparable" would continue to guide the force's engagement with the public, stressing that national security required shared responsibility.
"If the law is upheld effectively, all 34.3 million Malaysians will benefit," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BMI sees oil at US$66 in Q2
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Oil prices are expected to fall to US$66 per barrel in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2), following a stronger performance of US$78 per barrel in the first quarter (Q1), before gradually recovering towards year-end, according to BMI.
The research unit of Fitch Solutions said its forecast remains unchanged, with Brent crude projected at an annual average of US$70 per barrel for 2026.
The outlook assumes a relatively rapid post-conflict recovery, with limited lasting damage to critical upstream production and export infrastructure, as well as a two-to-four week normalisation of flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
"However, our country risk team notes rising risk of a longer-duration war, assigning a 35 per cent probability to their 'extend-to-end' scenario (lasting up to eight weeks) and a 25 per cent probability to their 'extend-to-escalate' scenario (lasting multiple months).
"If we move into the 'extend-to-end' scenario, we will raise our annual average forecast for Brent into a range of US$75-82 per barrel for 2026," it said in a statement.
BMI said it does not believe the disconnect between paper and physical markets can be sustained and expects greater contagion to Brent prices under the 'extend-to-end' scenario.
It also sees global market deficits ranging from 7.5 million to 15.0 million barrels per day.
"Market participants and policymakers have been pulling on a lot of levers to offset the losses from the Gulf, including pipeline rerouting away from the strait, strategic inventory releases, a drawdown of oil on water and commercial stocks onland, a temporary waiver of certain oil-related sanctions on Russia (and, to lesser effect, Iran), and an easing of some domestic fuel regulations," it added.
However, it said these buffers are rapidly being depleted, and there will be far fewer levers to pull in the next four weeks than in the previous four.
"Moreover, as the war continues, the threats to residual flows through the Strait of Hormuz and to the volumes transiting the Red Sea will rise, as will the risk of wider-spread damage to critical production and export infrastructure in the region, which could slow the post-conflict recovery," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran fires missile salvo after Trump signals progress in talks
Article:
TEHRAN: Iran launched a new missile salvo across the Middle East on Wednesday, after US President Donald Trump's signals that he was eager to talk with Teheran steadied global markets.
Oil prices dropped sharply and stocks in Asia rose on broader de-escalation hopes following nearly four weeks of war, as Trump appeared to be ramping up efforts to bring an end to his joint military operation with Israel.
Trump, whose daily statements have swung wildly from threatening to conciliatory, said Washington was "in negotiations right now" with Teheran.
"They did something yesterday that was amazing actually. They gave us a present and the present arrived today. And it was a very big present worth a tremendous amount of money," he told reporters in the Oval Office.
"That meant one thing to me – we're dealing with the right people."
The US leader did not explain further but said it related to the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has largely blockaded in retaliation for US and Israeli strikes, spiking global energy prices.
Iranian officials have yet to confirm any formal talks.
However, the New York Times – quoting several unnamed officials – reported that Washington has sent a 15-point plan to Iran via Pakistan, which has offered to mediate, in a bid to end the war.
It was not clear if Israel, which has been carrying out strikes on Iran and ally Hizbollah in Lebanon, was aware or on board with the plan.
Trump's cryptic statements on his eagerness to talk with Iran included repeating a claim that Tehran has "agreed they will never have a nuclear weapon."
Iran had agreed in 2015 to broad restraints on its contested nuclear programme in a deal that Trump ripped up during his first term as he joined Israel in applying pressure to the cleric-run state.
Despite the US leader's stated hopes for a deal, The Wall Street Journal reported that Washington is planning to send a further 3,000 soldiers to the Middle East.
Fresh Gulf attacks
While Trump hinted at backchannel diplomacy, Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had fired a fresh wave of missiles Wednesday at Israel, as well as bases hosting US forces in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.
[[nid:1403485]]
The Islamic republic has kept up its retaliatory attacks on Israel and Gulf nations it accuses of serving as launchpads for US strikes.
Drones hit a fuel tank and sparked a fire at Kuwait International Airport, the Gulf state's civil aviation authority said, causing "limited" damage.
In Bahrain, the interior ministry said air raid sirens were activated, while Jordan's public security directorate reported shrapnel fell near the capital Amman, resulting in no casualties or damage.
Saudi Arabia said it had intercepted at least four drones in the kingdom's east.
Israel meanwhile said it was launching new strikes on "infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime" after announcing new Iranian missile launches at the country.
Iran on Tuesday accused Israel of carrying out a second strike on Bushehr nuclear plant, a civilian site.
"The sounds, the explosions, the missiles – they are part of our daily life now," a 35-year-old woman in Tehran told AFP by telephone.
In Lebanon, state media said Israeli strikes killed six people in a town and a Palestinian refugee camp in the southern Sidon area.
There was no immediate comment from the Israeli military.
Israel has stepped up its campaign against the Iran-backed Hizbollah in Lebanon, saying its military would take control of south Lebanon up to the Litani river, around 30km (20 miles) from the border.
Trump's envoys were negotiating a nuclear deal with Iran just two days before the United States and Israel launched the massive attack on Feb 28, killing Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on the first day.
Lebanon was then pulled into the war when Hizbollah began firing rockets into Israel on March 2 to avenge Khamenei's killing.
The conflict has also pulled Iraq, which neighbours Iran, deeper into the war.
Baghdad said it would summon the US charge d'affaires and the Iranian ambassador after deadly strikes blamed on their countries, as Iraqi authorities granted targeted former paramilitary groups the "right to respond."
'Non-hostile vessels'
As the trading of strikes carried on, focus remained on the Strait of Hormuz, the key oil route responsible for carrying a fifth of the world's crude oil.
Tehran, in a message circulated by the International Maritime Organisation, assured safe passage through the strait to "non-hostile vessels."
Iran had already said it was not targeting friendly nations.
The IMO said a communique issued by Iran's foreign ministry said any vessels belonging to "the aggressor parties – namely the United States and the Israeli regime" would not be allowed passage.
Trump had earlier threatened to "obliterate" Iran's power plants, which some argue would be a war crime, if it did not open the strait by late Monday Washington time.
Before US markets opened Monday, Trump abruptly extended that deadline by five days, citing diplomatic progress.
But signs of a possible conclusion to the war saw benchmark price of crude oil dropped more than six percent.
Prices at the pump have soared in the US after Trump launched the war with Israel, causing him a political headache.
As the US leader mounted his threats if Iran did not agree to his demands, French President Emmanuel Macron demanded Teheran take any talks seriously.
"I called on Iran to engage in good faith in negotiations," he wrote on X, after a call with his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Population growth to drive 15pct rise in housing transactions: IQI
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's residential property transactions are projected to increase by 15 per cent over the next five years, driven primarily by population growth, according to IQI co-founder and group chief executive officer Kashif Ansari.
He said population expansion will lead to the formation of about 582,000 new households in that period, providing a strong underlying driver for housing demand nationwide.
"Population growth alone could lead to a 15 per cent increase in the number of homes purchased, and that leaves out demand from upgrade buyers, investors and foreign buyers," he said in a statement today.
Malaysia's population is expected to grow by 2.2 million people, from 34.3 million currently to 36.5 million by 2030.
Ansari said based on an average household size of 3.8 persons, the additional population would translate into more than 116,000 new households annually over the next five years.
This consistent household formation will continue to add both buyers and renters into the market, supporting transaction activity across the country, he said.
"Selangor is expected to remain the largest contributor to housing demand, with its population projected to increase by 382,000 by 2030, necessitating more transit-oriented, affordable and mid-market residential developments," he added.
Sabah is forecast to record the second-largest increase with an additional 283,000 residents, followed by Johor at 279,000, to benefit from stronger migration flows linked to the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone and the Rapid Transit System Link.
Kelantan and Terengganu are expected to register population growth of more than 12 per cent, highlighting rising housing needs in markets that have historically faced supply gaps.
Ansari added that the proportion of Malaysians living in urban areas is projected to rise to 79.3 per cent by 2030, driving a sustained need for housing, infrastructure and supporting services.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno Supreme Council to decide on former leaders' applications to return
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Umno Supreme Council will make the final decision on applications and appeals from former leaders and political figures seeking to rejoin the party, after a vetting process by the Rumah Bangsa Unification Committee.
Rumah Bangsa committee member Datuk Othman Desa said the panel held its first meeting earlier this month to establish guidelines and criteria for evaluating every application received.
"We expect to hold a second meeting after Aidilfitri to assess the applications and appeals.
"While I am uncertain of the exact number, we have begun receiving them, including from former leaders and certain prominent figures," he said today.
Othman, who is also the Umno Veteran chairman and a supreme council member, explained that the committee would scrutinise each case as some involve the jurisdiction of the disciplinary board and the supreme council.
"Some cases can be decided at the committee level, but those involving 'heavy' offences or serious disciplinary issues will be referred to the supreme council for a final decision," he said.
He added that while the committee provides recommendations to the supreme council on whether to accept or reject an applicant, the ultimate power lies with the party leadership.
It is understood that for members and leaders who left the party voluntarily without facing disciplinary action, their re-entry may be approved at the committee level.
However, for those who were sacked or suspended for breaching party discipline, their status will be referred directly to the supreme council.
Othman noted that the response has been encouraging since Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi expressed the party's openness to welcoming former members back.
"Some have even met the president personally to submit their applications. I see a very positive reception from former leaders and members," he added.
Among those reported to have submitted appeals or applications to return are former vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Umno youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin.
Others include former Jempol Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Mohd Sharif and former Tebrau Umno chief Datuk Maulizan Bujang.
Meanwhile, former Selangor Umno chairman Tan Sri Noh Omar, who recently left Bersatu, has expressed a willingness to return, albeit on the condition that Umno ceases its cooperation with DAP under the unity government.
However, former Umno information chief Shahril Hamdan, who was suspended in 2023, recently said it was not yet the right time for him to return, noting he currently prefers life outside of active politics.
Regarding reports that former Melaka chief minister Tan Sri Abdul Rahim Thamby Chik and former supreme council member Tan Sri Dr Rais Yatim—both of whom recently left Bersatu—might return to Umno, checks indicate that no formal applications have been received from them so far.
The establishment of "Rumah Bangsa" was agreed upon during the Umno General Assembly in January as a platform for former members to return to the fold, alongside the "Kolaborasi Agung" initiative aimed at uniting Malay-Muslim core parties in both sides of the house.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire losses exceed RM250mil since January
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Fire and Rescue Department has recorded fire-related losses exceeding RM250 million nationwide since January.
Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said that in the same period the department saved properties worth about RM816 million from being destroyed by fires.
He said the losses were recorded from 5,869 fire cases nationwide.
"Since January this year, we have recorded 5,869 fire cases, with Johor topping the list at 1,077 cases, followed by Kedah (984) and Selangor (906).
"During the same period, the department also saved properties worth about RM816 million from being razed," he said.
Nor Hisham said electrical short circuits were the main cause of fires, followed by gas leaks and the use of matches.
Meanwhile, he said three states recorded the highest number of road crashes during the Hari Raya period last year, namely Perak with 32 cases, Johor (21) and Pahang (11).
"During the festive season last year, a total of 142 road patrols were carried out by various agencies along 8,455km of road, particularly in accident-prone areas nationwide," he said.
Nor Hisham added that the department conducted 142 rescue operations last year, including two vehicle fire cases.
"During the operations, four people were killed, 19 injured and 12 others rescued," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple to be charged over alleged indecent act at Penang cemetery
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A couple arrested in connection with an alleged indecent act at a Chinese cemetery here will be charged at the magistrate's court tomorrow.
Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said the two individuals, aged 37 and 58, will be charged under Section 377D of the Penal Code.
The provision criminalises acts of "gross indecency" between persons, whether in public or private, and carries a penalty of up to two years' imprisonment.
"The couple, held in connection with the case, will be charged under Section 377D of the Penal Code at the George Town magistrate's court tomorrow," he said.
Police had earlier obtained a three-day remand order against the pair.
They were allegedly caught on video engaging in an indecent act at a Chinese cemetery.
Both individuals have admitted to committing the act.
Police began investigations
after receiving a report from a 69-year-old man regarding a video showing a man and a woman behaving inappropriately at the cemetery.
Initial findings showed the incident occurred on March 22 at the Batu Gantung Chinese cemetery in Air Itam.
The woman was arrested at 8.35pm on Monday at a flat near Jalan Padang Tembak, while the man was detained at 11.10pm in front of a building near Jalan Paya Terubong in Sungai Ara, Bayan Lepas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police smash 'Harraz Gang', four men nabbed for vehicle thefts, burglaries
Article:
MELAKA: Melaka police have successfully dismantled an organised crime group known as the "Harraz Gang", believed to be the mastermind behind a string of vehicle thefts, housebreaking and robberies in the Melaka Tengah district since the beginning of this year.
Melaka Tengah district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the success followed the arrest of four local men in a special operation, 'Op Lejang Melaka' carried out by the D4 (Intelligence/Operations) Division of the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) in Permatang Pasir, Alai, on March 19.
He said all the suspects, aged between 24 and 32, were arrested in less than 24 hours after a report of a motorcycle theft was received by the police.
"Initial investigations found that this group had been active since last January and was involved in at least 12 criminal cases involving motorcycle and van thefts as well as housebreaking and robbery cases in  Kandang, Bemban, Banda Hilir, and Tanjung Minyak areas.
"Their modus operandi is to target Yamaha 125ZR and Honda EX5 motorcycles using a modified 'master key' or T-bar, before disassembling the vehicle components to sell to third parties to eliminate evidence and make a source of income," he said at a press conference on the case at the Melaka police contingent headquarters (IPK) here today.
Christopher said a record check found that all the suspects were unemployed who had just been released from prison last October and had more than 20 serious criminal records related to theft, housebreaking and drugs.
Additionally, urine screening results also found that all of them tested positive for methamphetamine while two of the four suspects were on the wanted list.
In the operation, he said, police also seized six various types of motorcycles, a Nissan Vanette, as well as special equipment used to steal vehicles, with the value of the seized items estimated at more than RM50,000.
Christopher said the case is being investigated under Section 379A of the Penal Code for vehicle theft, Section 457 of the Penal Code for housebreaking and Section 392 of the Penal Code for robbery. All suspects are also being investigated under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
"All suspects are now remanded until tomorrow (March 26) to assist in further investigations. The police are also tracking down several other individuals who are believed to be linked to this group's network as buyers of stolen goods," he said.
In the meantime, he advised the public to always take extra security measures on their respective vehicles and park them in safe places to avoid any untoward incidents. - BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Globaltec's Australian unit flags no undisclosed info after shares double
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Globaltec Formation Bhd said its Australia-listed subsidiary, NuEnergy Gas Ltd, is not aware of any undisclosed information that could explain the surge in its share price and trading volume.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Globaltec said NuEnergy had responded to a price query from the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX), confirming that it had complied with disclosure requirements and that no material information had been withheld from the market.
NuEnergy's shares drew attention after more than doubling from A$0.039 on March 24 to an intraday high of A$0.087 on March 25, alongside a surge in trading volume, prompting a query from ASX.
In its response, NuEnergy pointed to positive independent analyst commentary published in Australian media as a possible factor behind the heightened investor interest.
The company also noted broader market interest in energy stocks, driven by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and its impact on global energy prices.
NuEnergy confirmed that it is in compliance with ASX listing rules, including continuous disclosure obligations, and that its response to the query had been approved by its board.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MoE directs schools to step up preparedness for hot weather
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: All educational institutions under the Education Ministry (MoE) have been instructed to enhance preparedness and take appropriate measures to cope with hot weather when schools reopen on Sunday and Monday.
The ministry said key measures include postponing outdoor activities if temperatures exceed 35 degrees Celsius and monitoring activities that may expose students to hot conditions to safeguard their health.
"Institutions must ensure an adequate supply of clean drinking water and encourage students to consume sufficient fluids to regulate body temperature," it said in a statement.
MoE also stressed that schools must provide drinking water to boarding students in critical situations where no other sources are available, and remain vigilant by coordinating with nearby clinics and hospitals in the event of heat-related health complications.
School management may also consider allowing students, teachers and support staff to wear appropriate sports attire during hot weather, depending on prevailing conditions.
The ministry emphasised that all institutions must remain alert to current hot weather conditions in their respective areas and comply with guidelines issued from time to time.
According to the academic calendar, Group A states — Kedah, Kelantan and Terengganu — are on holiday from March 20 to 28.
Group B states — Melaka, Johor, Negri Sembilan, Pahang, Perak, Perlis, Penang, Sabah, Sarawak, Selangor and the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur, Labuan and Putrajaya— are on holiday from March 21 to 29.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bau police issue two summonses to driver caught in viral dashcam clip for dangerous driving
Article:
KUCHING, March 25 — A driver was issued two traffic summonses after being caught on a viral dashcam video driving dangerously along Jalan Bau-Kuching near Kampung Keranji, Bau on Sunday.
Bau police chief Supt Mohd Haide A. Rahman said the incident, which occurred at about 5.34pm, involved a Perodua Kancil.
“The video, uploaded on a Facebook page, showed the vehicle entering the opposite lane and posing a danger to other road users.
“Following investigations and intelligence by the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division (BSPT) of Bau district police headquarters (IPD), the driver was successfully traced and instructed to present himself at BSPT IPD Bau for further action,” he said in a statement.
On March 24, at about 1.10pm, the driver turned up to assist in the investigation.
Mohd Haide said the driver admitted to driving while drowsy after attending a religious event in Kampung Blimbing, Bau, and was travelling from Bau towards Kuching.
The driver was found to have committed offences under Regulation 8 of the Road Traffic Rules LN167/59, namely crossing double lines and failing to keep to the correct lane. — The Borneo Post

Title: Home Minister says ‘corporate mafia’ probe ongoing, being carried out step by step
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Investigations into allegations of a “corporate mafia” are being conducted methodically, one step at a time, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today.
Speaking at a press conference after the 219th Police Day celebrations at the Police Training Centre (Pulapol) here, Saifuddin said an investigation paper has been opened, with authorities first working to establish the direction of the probe before identifying the modus operandi and individuals involved.
“The police are investigating under the Anti-Money Laundering Act (AMLA), but there is also another regulatory agency involved, namely the Securities Commission (SC), which has its own legislation that may also be applied in the investigation. Therefore, this inquiry will involve investigations under both the SC’s regulatory framework and AMLA,” he said.
“Let us proceed step by step. First, the AMLA team within the police will establish the direction of the investigation, after which the modus operandi will be identified. We will then reach out to those involved,” he added.
Saifuddin said any decision to restrict the travel documents of those involved, whether immediately or later, would be based on the findings of the investigation.
“I have been briefed by the Inspector-General of Police (IGP) and the team, and I am confident in their capability to carry out the investigation,” he said.
Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail had previously confirmed that businessman Victor Chin is linked to matters being investigated under AMLA. He added that Chin is currently believed to be abroad as authorities seek international cooperation to advance the probe.
The allegations surrounding the purported “corporate mafia” have drawn significant public attention following claims involving forced takeovers of public-listed companies and possible links to enforcement agencies, which Chin has denied. Chin has also claimed that a “Mr R” is involved in the process.

Title: Religious affairs minister warns Muslims against normalising tattoos, stresses need for expert guidance
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 25 — Muslims have been advised to uphold religious boundaries and personal dignity, and to avoid practices that contravene Islamic law, such as tattooing, which should not be normalised in society.
Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, in a statement today, reminded Muslims not to emulate actions that violate Islamic law.
“Muslims must always safeguard religious limits and personal dignity, including refraining from acts that contradict Islamic teachings, such as tattooing, which should not be normalised.
“As Muslims, we must preserve our faith and honour, and avoid exposing personal shortcomings on social media when facing difficulties. Instead, efforts should be made to seek appropriate help from qualified experts,” he said.
The Department of Islamic Development (Jakim), through its KSKCareCentre (KSKCC) at the Tuanku Mizan Zainal Abidin Mosque here, for example, provides free advisory services, syarie counselling and therapy, for such purposes.
Members of the public may also contact MyCareJAKIM via its hotline at 03-8870 7538 / 7460 / 7454 for assistance.
The statement follows a viral video involving a Muslim social media influencer and content creator who drew public attention after sharing a confession of having a tattoo on his left arm. — Bernama

Title: Former Sabah minister Peter Anthony granted parole, returns home after serving part of cheating sentence
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Former Sabah state minister Datuk Peter Anthony, who is about a year into serving a three-year sentence for a cheating case at Kajang Prison, has reportedly been released on parole and returned home in the interior of Sabah.
Deputy president of Parti Kesejahteraan Demokratik Masyarakat (KDM), Datuk Wetrom Bahanda, confirmed Peter’s release but declined to provide further details.
No official confirmation has been obtained from the authorities, but Prison Department sources said he applied for parole as soon as he was eligible and was granted conditional parole. It is believed he was released on March 13 and returned to Sabah last week.
Sabah police commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikon said parole matters fall under the Prison Department and declined to elaborate. Under amendments to the Prisons Act 1995 (Amendment 2008), eligible inmates may serve the remainder of their sentence outside prison once granted parole.
Peter was charged with committing fraud between June 13 and August 21, 2014, at the Prime Minister’s Department in Putrajaya, under Section 468 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum sentence of seven years’ imprisonment and a fine.
The charge stated that, as managing director of Asli Jati Sdn Bhd, he had forged a letter dated June 9, 2014, from the office of the vice-chancellor of Universiti Malaysia Sabah, with intent to deceive. The letter was related to a maintenance and services contract for the university’s mechanical and electrical systems.
The Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court convicted him in May 2022, sentencing him to three years in prison and a RM50,000 fine. His appeals to the High Court and Court of Appeal were dismissed, and he began serving his sentence on March 4, 2025.
Peter was elected as the Melalap assemblyman under Parti Warisan in the 2018 general election and was later appointed Sabah Infrastructure Development Minister. He left Warisan in 2022 and formed Parti Kesejahteraan Demokratik Masyarakat (KDM). Following his imprisonment, his daughter Priscella took over as acting party president. In the last Sabah state election, the party contested 40 seats but won only one, while Priscella lost the Melalap seat she contested in place of her father.

Title: MetMalaysia: Inter‑monsoon phase to bring severe weather nationwide, with thunderstorms and possible hail
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The Monsoon Transition Phase is expected to begin this Sunday (March 29) and continue until May, leading to thunderstorms and heavy rains accompanied by strong winds in most areas in a short spell.
Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said the areas expected to be affected are the west and interior of the Peninsular Malaysia, south and central Sarawak and the western part of Sabah.
He said the phase would continue until May, marking the end of the 2025/2026 Northeast Monsoon which began on Nov 13 last year.
He said during that period, the country would receive weak winds from various directions which could potentially encourage the formation of thunderstorm clouds.
“This condition usually leads to heavy rains accompanied by strong winds in a short period of time, especially in the evening and early night,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Hisham said the weather phenomenon also has the potential to generate tornadoes and produce hailstones, in addition to causing flash floods, landslides, water surges, fallen trees, lightning strikes and damage to unstable structures.
In this regard, the public is advised to take shelter inside houses or buildings, stay away from open areas, water reservoirs, tall structures such as trees and towers, electrical conductor rods including electric poles and iron ladders.
The public is also advised to always be aware of weather forecasts and warnings issued by MetMalaysia via the official website, myCuaca mobile application and the department’s official social media.
For further enquiries, the public can contact the department’s hotline at 1-300-22-1638. — Bernama

Title: Drought disaster declaration lifted in Tawau, water supply in Sebatik still monitored
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 25 — Authorities have lifted the drought disaster declaration in Tawau following improved conditions, although water supply issues in Pulau Sebatik remain under close watch.
In a statement, Tawau District Disaster Management Committee (JPBD) chairman Joseph Pang said the decision was made after recent assessments showed the situation no longer meets the criteria for a drought disaster.
“Overall conditions in the district are now under control, marking a shift from earlier concerns over prolonged dry weather,” he said in a statement here today.
An earlier declaration was issued at 10am today but was retracted at about 1.30pm, Pang added.
However, he noted that water supply shortages in Pulau Sebatik are still being closely monitored by authorities.
“Continuous monitoring will be carried out, and appropriate action will be taken to ensure the situation remains under control,” he said.
The committee also advised the public to stay updated through official channels and practise prudent water usage to prevent further strain on supply.
The earlier drought declaration had raised concerns over water availability in parts of Tawau, particularly in outlying areas such as Sebatik Island, which are more vulnerable to supply disruptions.

Title: PM joins top leadership at 219th Police Day celebration at Pulapol, launches children’s crime‑awareness book
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today attended the 219th Police Day celebration at the Police Training Centre (PULAPOL) in Kuala Lumpur.
He arrived at about 2 pm and was received by Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail, his deputy Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay and Bukit Aman police directors.
Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
At the event, the Prime Minister is scheduled to launch the book,
Buku MESRA PDRM-Modul Kesedaran Jenayah Kanak-Kanak dan Remaja
.
Police Day, themed “Police and the Community Are Inseparable”, is observed annually on March 25 to honour the service, sacrifices and dedication of police personnel in maintaining national security and public order.
The date also marks the historic establishment of the police force on March 25, 1807, in Penang, following the Charter of Justice. — Bernama

Title: Anwar warns Malaysia cannot take national security for granted amid global tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that escalating global tensions, including the ongoing conflict involving Iran, should serve as a reminder that Malaysia cannot afford to take its national security for granted.
Speaking at the 219th Police Day celebrations at the Police Training Centre (Pulapol) here, Anwar said the situation was complex and required careful understanding, particularly by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), given the potential security implications arising from global instability.
“In a situation of global turmoil, we must not assume that we are immune or overly comfortable simply because the country remains peaceful and stable. Malaysia is among the nations with strong growth potential and relative stability, which reassures our people, but this should not cause us to take lightly the emerging security challenges,” he said.
Anwar added that signs of desperation could surface at various levels amid international conflicts, stressing the need for authorities to remain vigilant and better prepared.
He said the caution was informed by briefings and exchanges with international counterparts, as well as insights from Malaysia’s intelligence agencies.
“I share this because I have also received explanations and perspectives from our international partners and intelligence bodies. We must not view national security issues lightly in times of such turmoil,” he said.
Anwar stressed that PDRM must continue strengthening its preparedness to address any potential threats, noting that developments abroad could have indirect repercussions on domestic security.
His remarks come amid heightened geopolitical tensions linked to the conflict involving Iran, which have raised global concerns over potential economic disruptions and security risks.

Title: Govt to roll out proactive interventions to safeguard economic resilience, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — The government will immediately implement several proactive and thorough early intervention measures to maintain the country’s economic resilience amid the conflict in West Asia.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said this was among the matters decided when chairing the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting today to examine the latest geopolitical developments, global economic pressures and security implications following the conflict.
Anwar said that the government is also mobilising efforts to ensure the security of energy supply, including diversifying sources and exploring alternative routes to reduce dependence on risky routes, such as the Strait of Hormuz.
“Although still in the festive Aidilfitri atmosphere, the people’s well-being, particularly the cost of living, remains the Madani Government’s priority,” he posted on Facebook.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the government would continue to closely monitor the conflict situation in West Asia, with information channelled directly to the public.
In facing the challenge, the Prime Minister said calmness, discipline and a spirit of togetherness were crucial to ensuring the country remained stable and resilient. — Bernama

Title: Shoplot owner ordered to pay TNB RM26,000 over tampered electricity meter
Article:
KLANG, March 25 — A sessions court here has ordered shoplot owner Choo Lee Chen to pay Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) RM26,146.70 for an “undercharged” electricity bill, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT).
According to the report, TNB filed the suit in 2024 over alleged irregular electricity usage at Choo’s shoplot in Kota Bayu Emas Pendamar, Port Klang, claiming it was linked to Bitcoin mining.
Officers inspecting the unit in July 2020 discovered that the meter had been tampered with, with wiring from the incoming supply isolator bypassing the meter to a distribution box.
Choo argued she rented the shoplot to a company and that the tenant should be responsible for electricity usage. TNB had initially sought RM61,310.05 from her for the underpaid bills.
Sessions Court judge Noorhisham Jaafar clarified the calculation and held Choo liable as the registered consumer, saying she had a contractual relationship with TNB for electricity supply.
“As a matter of general principle, contractual and statutory obligations under the Electricity Supply Act attach to the registered consumer unless and until the account is lawfully transferred or terminated,” the judge said, according to FMT.
Noorhisham also noted that the tenant moved out in April 2019, about a year before TNB’s inspection, and that Choo had taken no action against the firm, including lodging a police report or civil proceedings.
He added that the lack of documentary proof or action “sits uneasily with the stance now adopted in these proceedings, particularly given that vacant possession was already returned to her more than a year before the inspection,” FMT reported.

Title: Who is Mr R? PKR’s Ramanan dares Victor Chin to name MP given RM9.5m
Article:
SUNGAI BULOH, March 25 — PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan today dismissed insinuations that he is the lawmaker alleged to have received RM9 million from businessman Victor Chin, who is implicated in the so-called “Corporate Mafia” controversy.
Ramanan challenged Chin to reveal the identity of “Mr. R”, while also taking aim at former party deputy Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, suggesting he was trying to capitalise on Chin’s “sensational” allegations to attack the party.
“Only Chin can answer who Mr. R is,” Ramanan said at a press conference he held this afternoon.
MORE TO COME

Title: Saravanan says MIC staying with BN for now, will study Indian sentiment on PAS-led Perikatan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — MIC will remain in Barisan Nasional (BN) for now, its deputy president Datuk Seri M. Saravanan said, but stopped short of ruling out a future move to Perikatan Nasional (PN), according to
Free Malaysia Today (FMT).
“We are staying with BN for now,” he said at a press conference at the party’s headquarters today.
Asked if this meant MIC was ruling out joining PN, Saravanan replied: “No, I’m not saying that,” according to the news portal.
Saravanan said recent developments within PN require MIC to reassess its position, particularly following changes in the coalition’s leadership.
“PN has undergone some changes. It was led by Bersatu when we enquired. Now it is led by PAS,” he was quoted as saying.
He added that the party must consider grassroots views and the sentiment of the Indian community before joining a PAS-led coalition.
He also said MIC has not rejected PN outright, noting that earlier discussions with the coalition took place under a different leadership structure.
Saravanan’s remarks came a week after he dismissed claims by PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan that MIC had officially joined the coalition.
Talk of MIC leaving BN has been ongoing since the party’s general assembly in November, when delegates gave the leadership a mandate to consider such a move amid dissatisfaction over the party’s role and relevance, FMT reported.

Title: Rafizi says ‘it’s not me’ amid RM9.5m PKR MP claim, jokes only Aidilfitri weight gained in Instagram post
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 25 — Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations linking him to claims that a PKR MP received RM9.5 million to resolve a corporate mafia-related issue, saying “it’s not me” in a brief Instagram post.
The former PKR deputy president responded with humour, saying the only thing he had “received” during the Aidilfitri period was an increase in body weight rather than any cash, as alleged.
“But since I’m still in the festive mood, I will comment tomorrow instead,” he said in the Instagram post.
The response comes amid growing controversy following allegations by businessman Victor Chin Boon Loong, who in a 40-page document detailed a chronology of events claiming a PKR MP had demanded RM10 million to settle a corporate mafia-related matter.
According to various media reports, Chin alleged he was only able to provide RM9.5 million initially, while the remaining RM500,000 was never handed over as the issue was not resolved as promised.
The corporate mafia allegations centre on claims that senior officials from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) orchestrated intimidation campaigns and pressured executives and shareholders of listed companies to resign or divest their stakes at discounted prices to connected businessmen.
MACC has denied the allegations, describing them as an attempt to undermine the credibility of its investigations and enforcement actions, and said the claims appeared to originate from an anonymous online blog lacking clear authorship or accountability.
Separately, police confirmed that several premises linked to Victor Chin in Kemensah and Sungai Long in Kajang were raided on March 16 to assist investigations under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act, Malaysiakini reported

Title: Melaka police arrest four, cripple Harraz Gang behind spate of vehicle thefts and burglaries
Article:
MELAKA, March 25 — Melaka police have successfully dismantled an organised crime group known as the “Harraz Gang”, believed to be the mastermind behind a string of vehicle thefts, housebreaking and robberies in the Melaka Tengah district since the beginning of this year.
Melaka Tengah district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the success followed the arrest of four local men in a special operation, ‘Op Lejang Melaka’ carried out by the D4 (Intelligence/Operations) Division of the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) in Permatang Pasir, Alai, on March 19.
He said all the suspects, aged between 24 and 32, were arrested in less than 24 hours after a report of a motorcycle theft was received by the police.
“Initial investigations found that this group had been active since last January and was involved in at least 12 criminal cases involving motorcycle and van thefts as well as housebreaking and robbery cases in Kandang, Bemban, Banda Hilir, and Tanjung Minyak areas.
“Their modus operandi is to target Yamaha 125ZR and Honda EX5 motorcycles using a modified ‘master key’ or T-bar, before disassembling the vehicle components to sell to third parties to eliminate evidence and make a source of income,” he said at a press conference on the case at the Melaka police contingent headquarters (IPK) here today.
Christopher said a record check found that all the suspects were unemployed who had just been released from prison last October and had more than 20 serious criminal records related to theft, housebreaking and drugs.
Additionally, urine screening results also found that all of them tested positive for methamphetamine while two of the four suspects were on the wanted list.
In the operation, he said, police also seized six various types of motorcycles, a Nissan Vanette, as well as special equipment used to steal vehicles, with the value of the seized items estimated at more than RM50,000.
Christopher said the case is being investigated under Section 379A of the Penal Code for vehicle theft, Section 457 of the Penal Code for housebreaking and Section 392 of the Penal Code for robbery. All suspects are also being investigated under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
“All suspects are now remanded until tomorrow (March 26) to assist in further investigations. The police are also tracking down several other individuals who are believed to be linked to this group’s network as buyers of stolen goods,” he said.
In the meantime, he advised the public to always take extra security measures on their respective vehicles and park them in safe places to avoid any untoward incidents. — Bernama
